title: Prototype session-to-session compare views in TSX
summary: Began a compare-view concept so operators can inspect two frontend sessions side by side and understand overlap without manually hopping between expanded cards.
goal: Move the dashboard closer to L4 observability by making side-by-side session comparison a real TSX workflow.
changesMade: Mapped the compare use cases coming from hotspot and conflict review
Outlined a split-pane TSX layout for paired session context
Identified the data slices needed for files, rationale, and test histories
Reviewed how compare actions could originate from conflicts and session cards

features.name: paired session compare view
features.why: Operators need a direct way to inspect overlap and divergence between two frontend sessions.

features.name: compare data shaping
features.why: A compare view depends on consistent session payloads and highlighted deltas.

features.name: conflict-origin compare entry points
features.why: Conflict review is the most obvious place to launch side-by-side inspection.

decisions.decision: Start with same-repo session compare instead of cross-repo compare
decisions.why: The existing handoff data model is repo-local, so this keeps scope aligned with what the system already knows.

decisions.decision: Compare rationale and files before raw patch text
decisions.why: Intent and touched surfaces are faster to scan than long markdown patches.

blockers: Need a compare state model and likely URL params before the split-pane view can be navigated reliably.
openQuestions: Should compare mode live inside the current shell or open a dedicated route?
resumePrompt: Continue the compare prototype by settling the paired-session data shape in dashboard/src/lib/data.ts before touching the TSX layout.
